steps.title: Identify the alignment
steps.content: The strongest clue is that nautical miles directly equal one minute (1/60th degree) of latitude. This precise structural correspondence is the deciding factor.

steps.title: Connect the reasoning
steps.content:
C establishes the full causal chain: because nautical miles correspond to coordinates on nautical charts, and those coordinates account for Earth's curvature, nautical miles enable efficient distance calculation.

A and B mention relevant information without completing the connection, while D claims latitude/longitude doesn't account for curvature—contradicting the notes.

This matches choice C — C is correct.
